title: Package Name Prefixes
excerpt: django plugins, cloud SDKs, and 1,999 homework submissions.
content_html:
PEP 752 was accepted at the end of June. It lets an organisation apply to PyPI to reserve a package name prefix, after which new uploads matching that prefix are rejected unless they come from the grant holder. Existing packages are grandfathered. The motivation is impersonation: someone publishing malware as google-cloud-something or apache-something gets the vendor’s name in front of it. The PEP names four example prefixes: google-cloud-, opentelemetry-, apache-airflow-providers-, and types-.

I pulled every package name from PyPI (912,665 of them, via ecosyste.ms) and counted prefixes. Those four account for 264, 271, 154, and 1,295 packages respectively, and each is published almost entirely by one organisation. Google publishes google-cloud-*, the OpenTelemetry project publishes opentelemetry-*, the Apache Airflow team publishes apache-airflow-providers-*, and the typeshed maintainers publish types-*. Prefix reservation formalises what those publishers already do; one of the PEP’s authors, Jarek Potiuk, is on the Airflow PMC.

There are 13,934 prefixes on PyPI shared by five or more packages. The biggest is django-* with 17,450, published by thousands of different authors at a rate of about a thousand a year since 2010. flask-* has 2,815, pytest-* has 2,137, mkdocs-* has 764, sphinxcontrib-* has 370. collective.*, the Plone community’s shared prefix using Python’s namespace package machinery, has 1,436 packages and has been in use since at least 2008. Anyone publishes a Django app as django-something, a pytest plugin as pytest-something, a Sphinx extension as sphinxcontrib-something, and the host project’s documentation tells them to.

An earlier draft of PEP 752 had a mode for this, letting a grant holder mark a prefix as open so that anyone could still publish under it while the grant holder’s own packages got a visual indicator. It was removed as having “insufficient motivation and the fact that repositories could technically satisfy such use cases with standard grant semantics.” Multiple organisations can share a grant, which handles a prefix split across a few organisations. Under the accepted design, if the Django Software Foundation reserved django-, the existing 17,450 packages would keep publishing new versions and the roughly one thousand new django-* packages that would otherwise arrive next year would be blocked. I compared namespace designs across registries earlier this year; NuGet, crates.io and npm have each attached something to prefixes already.

Seven registries

Splitting package names on runs of -, _ and . and grouping by the first token:

  packages prefixes with ≥5 packages packages with a shared prefix ≥100 ≥500
pypi.org 912,665 13,934 41% 427 66
crates.io 311,301 9,161 54% 166 19
rubygems.org 209,853 4,214 46% 132 18
hex.pm 22,373 382 32% 12 1
hackage.haskell.org 19,345 521 41% 9 0
npmjs.org (unscoped) 2,678,188 37,460 68% 2,604 468
nuget.org 828,361 22,377 70% 690 72

npm has had @scope/name since 2014 so its row counts only the 63% of packages that are unscoped. Between a third and two-thirds of every registry’s packages share a first token with at least four others. The counts here are first-token only; multi-token prefixes like apache-airflow-providers- are counted under apache-* in the table and separately when named in the text.

Open plugin conventions, where many authors publish extensions named after a host project, are the largest category among the biggest prefixes on every registry: django-* (17,450 pypi), react-* (86,142 npm), jekyll-* (1,658 rubygems), bevy-* (1,870 crates), phoenix_* (245 hex), servant-* (152 hackage). Some of these are functional, meaning the name has to match the pattern for the host tool to load the plugin: cargo runs cargo-foo as the cargo foo subcommand, ESLint’s config resolver matches eslint-plugin-foo, pytest loads pytest-foo via entry points.

Generated SDK families, where one organisation publishes hundreds of packages from an API spec, are the next largest. aws-sdk-* (473 crates, 481 gems, 92 pypi), google-cloud-* (258 crates, 510 gems, 264 pypi, 5 hackage), azure-mgmt-* (273 crates, 124 gems, 334 pypi). The Odoo Community Association publishes about 20,000 packages on PyPI across seven versioned prefix trees (odoo8-addon-* through odoo14-addon-* plus odoo-addon-*), which is the largest coordinated prefix use I found on any registry. On npm the generated families are the ones that adopt scopes: @types/* 11,388, @stdlib/* 5,555, @fontsource/* 2,122.

Language and framework tags account for another large block: python-* (6,278), rust-* (1,692), ex_* (1,123 hex, the Elixir convention), node-* (23,389 npm), is-* (3,754 npm, predicate functions), use-* (4,317 npm, React hooks). Hackage has a set of these that mirror module paths (data-*, network-*, language-*).

Getting-started guides produce their own prefixes: hola-* on rubygems has 764 packages from people following the make your own gem guide, example-package-* and example-pkg-* on PyPI have 1,031 between them from the packaging.python.org tutorial, and guessing-game-* on crates.io has 125 from chapter 2 of the Rust book. topsis-* on PyPI has 1,999 packages with student roll numbers embedded in the names (TOPSIS is a decision-analysis method that gets set as a programming assignment), from what appears to be a university course run each year since 2020.

Bulk publication accounts for some of the largest recent prefixes: use-* on crates.io has 820 packages, 809 of them published in 2026 by one account. iflow-mcp-* on PyPI has 2,505 packages of republished MCP servers from one publisher, plus 5,184 more under @iflow-mcp on npm from the same account. free.robux-* on NuGet has 3,620. mcp-* on PyPI, from many publishers, went from 2 packages in 2023 to 3,022 by mid-2026, reaching that count faster than django-* reached 3,000.

For the top 1,000 packages by downloads, between 69% and 80% on crates.io, rubygems, and pypi either belong to a prefix cluster or are the package a large cluster is named after (serde, pytest, rack); hex is 45% and hackage 60%. Of the packages flagged critical on ecosyste.ms, 226 of rubygems’ 974 are aws-sdk-* alone, and 74% of NuGet’s are under Microsoft.* or System.*.

NuGet

PEP 752 cites NuGet’s ID prefix reservation as prior art. It went live in October 2017, works the same way (apply by email, matching uploads are rejected unless from the reservation holder, existing packages grandfathered), and adds a checkmark to verified packages in the gallery and Visual Studio. NuGet also supports subprefix delegation, where the Microsoft.* holder can delegate Microsoft.AspNet.* to a different account, and public reservations, where the holder gets the checkmark on their own packages while other publishers can still upload under the prefix, which is what PEP 752’s earlier draft called an open namespace.

Sampling NuGet’s per-package verified flag via the search API across the top 30 non-spam prefixes shows Microsoft.*, DevExpress.*, Syncfusion.* and JetBrains.* fully verified and single-publisher, and Google.*, Amazon.* and Azure.* mostly verified with some grandfathered community packages.

Serilog.* is 6% verified across 776 packages, and Serilog is a logging library with a plugin model where sinks and enrichers are published as Serilog.Sinks.Foo and Serilog.Enrichers.Foo by whoever writes them. Sampling 60 of those and checking first-publish dates against verified, the core team’s packages (Serilog, Serilog.Sinks.File) are verified and community packages aren’t, including community packages first published in every year through 2025 (Serilog.Enrichers.Metrics.Memory, September 2025, owner Jandini). That is consistent with Serilog.* being a public reservation: community sinks are still being accepted eight years in. Cake.* (607 packages, Cake build addins) shows the same pattern at 7% verified. The checkmark appears per package; the reservation list itself is private to NuGet.

crates.io

Cargo’s RFC 3243, accepted and currently being implemented, takes a different approach: whoever owns the foo crate can publish foo::bar, and only they can, with no application process. Of the 166 crates.io prefixes with 100 or more packages, 156 have a bare crate matching the first token, and of those 37% last released more than five years ago and 20% have exactly one version. The async crate has two versions, last release 2014, while 844 crates are named async-*. The google, aws and tree crates are each owned by individuals with no connection to Google, AWS, or tree-sitter. bevy, serde, tokio, tauri, actix, axum, and windows are owned by their respective projects. RFC 3243 explicitly avoids touching the existing hyphenated names (serde_json stays where it is; serde::json would be a new crate), so the 702 serde-* crates from many authors continue alongside anything the serde owners publish under serde::.

npm

npm added @scope/name in 2014, and 37% of the 4.26 million live packages use one. Among the unscoped 63%, 68% share a prefix with at least four other packages. For the flat prefixes that also have a matching scope, the flat side is almost always larger, with the core team publishing under the scope and the community under the flat prefix: babel-* 6,099 vs @babel/* 239, vue-* 23,871 vs @vue/* 110, eslint-* 16,664 vs @eslint/* 17. The exceptions are generated families that migrated or started scoped, like @types/* (11,388) and @stdlib/* (5,555). eslint-plugin-* (6,957) and babel-plugin-* (3,987) stay flat because the tools string-match those prefixes when resolving config.

Bulk publication appears in scopes as well as flat names: @hyper.fun alone has 30,273 packages, alongside a run of scopes with random-string names ending in npm (@wemnyelezxnpm, @diahkomalasarinpm, @ryniaubenpm) holding 1,500-1,900 packages each, all created in April 2024 during the tea.xyz protocol spam wave. dsr-* (10,742) and tea-* (4,486) from the same wave are unscoped.

Browsing

The projects with the biggest plugin conventions maintain their own directories: jekyllrb.com/docs/plugins, homebridge.io, flow.nodered.org, n8n.io/integrations, fastlane’s plugin list. These are curated, so a package is listed because the project added it. Registry search returns fuzzy-ranked results across the whole index, so searching rubygems.org for jekyll gives popular jekyll-related gems mixed with anything else that mentions the word. NuGet’s checkmark and npm’s npmjs.com/org/babel scope page are the two places a registry currently exposes something membership-like.

A prefix page that lists every jekyll-* gem ranked by dependent count, with jekyll itself pinned and its direct owners’ packages marked, would get most of the way to what those project directories provide, and the data to build it is already in the registry index. Third-party registry browsers like npmx are well placed to try it for npm’s flat prefixes, and I have the cross-registry data on ecosyste.ms so I might build one there.

PEP 755

PEP 752’s four named prefixes cover about 2,000 PyPI packages from one organisation each, against about 25,000 under the open-convention prefixes named above. The companion policy PEP 755 is still in draft and sets out who can apply for a grant and on what terms, including a proposed queue priority for paid organisations. How it handles an application for django- or pytest-, from the project or from anyone else, is still to be worked out, and NuGet’s handling of Serilog.* is one existing precedent.

Data and scripts: github.com/andrew/package-name-prefixes.

blocks.html_en: Open plugin conventions, where many authors publish extensions named after a host project, are the largest category among the biggest prefixes on every registry: django-* (17,450 pypi), react-* (86,142 npm), jekyll-* (1,658 rubygems), bevy-* (1,870 crates), phoenix_* (245 hex), servant-* (152 hackage). Some of these are functional, meaning the name has to match the pattern for the host tool to load the plugin: cargo runs cargo-foo as the cargo foo subcommand, ESLint’s config resolver matches eslint-plugin-foo, pytest loads pytest-foo via entry points.

sentences.en: Open plugin conventions, where many authors publish extensions named after a host project, are the largest category among the biggest prefixes on every registry: django-* (17,450 pypi), react-* (86,142 npm), jekyll-* (1,658 rubygems), bevy-* (1,870 crates), phoenix_* (245 hex), servant-* (152 hackage).
sentences.zh: 开放插件约定（许多作者发布以主机项目命名的扩展）是每个注册表中最大前缀中最大的类别：django-* (17,450 pypi)、react-* (86,142 npm)、jekyll-* (1,658 rubygems)、bevy-* (1,870 crates)、phoenix_* (245 hex)、servant-* (152)黑客）。

sentences.en: Some of these are functional, meaning the name has to match the pattern for the host tool to load the plugin: cargo runs cargo-foo as the cargo foo subcommand, ESLint’s config resolver matches eslint-plugin-foo, pytest loads pytest-foo via entry points.
sentences.zh: 其中一些是功能性的，这意味着名称必须与主机工具加载插件的模式相匹配：cargo 将cargo-foo 作为cargo foo 子命令运行，ESLint 的配置解析器匹配eslint-plugin-foo，pytest 通过入口点加载pytest-foo。

blocks.html_en: Generated SDK families, where one organisation publishes hundreds of packages from an API spec, are the next largest. aws-sdk-* (473 crates, 481 gems, 92 pypi), google-cloud-* (258 crates, 510 gems, 264 pypi, 5 hackage), azure-mgmt-* (273 crates, 124 gems, 334 pypi). The Odoo Community Association publishes about 20,000 packages on PyPI across seven versioned prefix trees (odoo8-addon-* through odoo14-addon-* plus odoo-addon-*), which is the largest coordinated prefix use I found on any registry. On npm the generated families are the ones that adopt scopes: @types/* 11,388, @stdlib/* 5,555, @fontsource/* 2,122.

blocks.html_en: Getting-started guides produce their own prefixes: hola-* on rubygems has 764 packages from people following the make your own gem guide, example-package-* and example-pkg-* on PyPI have 1,031 between them from the packaging.python.org tutorial, and guessing-game-* on crates.io has 125 from chapter 2 of the Rust book. topsis-* on PyPI has 1,999 packages with student roll numbers embedded in the names (TOPSIS is a decision-analysis method that gets set as a programming assignment), from what appears to be a university course run each year since 2020.

blocks.html_en: For the top 1,000 packages by downloads, between 69% and 80% on crates.io, rubygems, and pypi either belong to a prefix cluster or are the package a large cluster is named after (serde, pytest, rack); hex is 45% and hackage 60%. Of the packages flagged critical on ecosyste.ms, 226 of rubygems’ 974 are aws-sdk-* alone, and 74% of NuGet’s are under Microsoft.* or System.*.

sentences.en: For the top 1,000 packages by downloads, between 69% and 80% on crates.io, rubygems, and pypi either belong to a prefix cluster or are the package a large cluster is named after (serde, pytest, rack); hex is 45% and hackage 60%.

blocks.html_en: PEP 752 cites NuGet’s ID prefix reservation as prior art. It went live in October 2017, works the same way (apply by email, matching uploads are rejected unless from the reservation holder, existing packages grandfathered), and adds a checkmark to verified packages in the gallery and Visual Studio. NuGet also supports subprefix delegation, where the Microsoft.* holder can delegate Microsoft.AspNet.* to a different account, and public reservations, where the holder gets the checkmark on their own packages while other publishers can still upload under the prefix, which is what PEP 752’s earlier draft called an open namespace.

sentences.en: PEP 752 cites NuGet’s ID prefix reservation as prior art.
sentences.zh: PEP 752 引用 NuGet 的 ID 前缀预留作为现有技术。

sentences.en: It went live in October 2017, works the same way (apply by email, matching uploads are rejected unless from the reservation holder, existing packages grandfathered), and adds a checkmark to verified packages in the gallery and Visual Studio.
sentences.zh: 它于 2017 年 10 月上线，工作方式相同（通过电子邮件申请，除非来自预订持有者，否则匹配的上传将被拒绝，现有包将不受限制），并在库和 Visual Studio 中为经过验证的包添加复选标记。

sentences.en: NuGet also supports subprefix delegation, where the Microsoft.* holder can delegate Microsoft.AspNet.* to a different account, and public reservations, where the holder gets the checkmark on their own packages while other publishers can still upload under the prefix, which is what PEP 752’s earlier draft called an open namespace.
sentences.zh: NuGet 还支持子前缀委托（其中 Microsoft.* 持有者可以将 Microsoft.AspNet.* 委托给其他帐户）和公共保留（其中持有者在自己的包上打勾，而其他发布者仍然可以在前缀下上传，这就是 PEP 752 的早期草案所谓的开放命名空间）。

blocks.html_en: Sampling NuGet’s per-package verified flag via the search API across the top 30 non-spam prefixes shows Microsoft.*, DevExpress.*, Syncfusion.* and JetBrains.* fully verified and single-publisher, and Google.*, Amazon.* and Azure.* mostly verified with some grandfathered community packages.

sentences.en: Sampling NuGet’s per-package verified flag via the search API across the top 30 non-spam prefixes shows Microsoft.*, DevExpress.*, Syncfusion.* and JetBrains.* fully verified and single-publisher, and Google.*, Amazon.* and Azure.* mostly verified with some grandfathered community packages.
sentences.zh: 通过搜索 API 在前 30 个非垃圾邮件前缀中对 NuGet 的每个包验证标志进行采样，结果显示 Microsoft.*、DevExpress.*、Syncfusion.* 和 JetBrains.* 已完全验证且是单一发布商，而 Google.*、Amazon.* 和 Azure.* 则主要通过一些旧社区包进行验证。

blocks.html_en: Serilog.* is 6% verified across 776 packages, and Serilog is a logging library with a plugin model where sinks and enrichers are published as Serilog.Sinks.Foo and Serilog.Enrichers.Foo by whoever writes them. Sampling 60 of those and checking first-publish dates against verified, the core team’s packages (Serilog, Serilog.Sinks.File) are verified and community packages aren’t, including community packages first published in every year through 2025 (Serilog.Enrichers.Metrics.Memory, September 2025, owner Jandini). That is consistent with Serilog.* being a public reservation: community sinks are still being accepted eight years in. Cake.* (607 packages, Cake build addins) shows the same pattern at 7% verified. The checkmark appears per package; the reservation list itself is private to NuGet.

sentences.en: Serilog.* is 6% verified across 776 packages, and Serilog is a logging library with a plugin model where sinks and enrichers are published as Serilog.Sinks.Foo and Serilog.Enrichers.Foo by whoever writes them.
sentences.zh: Serilog.* 在 776 个包中得到了 6% 的验证，Serilog 是一个带有插件模型的日志库，其中接收器和丰富器由编写它们的人发布为 Serilog.Sinks.Foo 和 Serilog.Enrichers.Foo。

sentences.zh: 对其中的 60 个进行抽样并检查首次发布日期是否经过验证，核心团队的软件包（Serilog、Serilog.Sinks.File）经过验证，而社区软件包则未经验证，包括截至 2025 年每年首次发布的社区软件包（Serilog.Enrichers.Metrics.Memory，2025 年 9 月，所有者 Jandini）。

sentences.en: Cake.* (607 packages, Cake build addins) shows the same pattern at 7% verified.

sentences.en: crates.io

blocks.html_en: Cargo’s RFC 3243, accepted and currently being implemented, takes a different approach: whoever owns the foo crate can publish foo::bar, and only they can, with no application process. Of the 166 crates.io prefixes with 100 or more packages, 156 have a bare crate matching the first token, and of those 37% last released more than five years ago and 20% have exactly one version. The async crate has two versions, last release 2014, while 844 crates are named async-*. The google, aws and tree crates are each owned by individuals with no connection to Google, AWS, or tree-sitter. bevy, serde, tokio, tauri, actix, axum, and windows are owned by their respective projects. RFC 3243 explicitly avoids touching the existing hyphenated names (serde_json stays where it is; serde::json would be a new crate), so the 702 serde-* crates from many authors continue alongside anything the serde owners publish under serde::.

sentences.en: Cargo’s RFC 3243, accepted and currently being implemented, takes a different approach: whoever owns the foo crate can publish foo::bar, and only they can, with no application process.
sentences.zh: Cargo 的 RFC 3243 已被接受并正在实施，采用了不同的方法：拥有 foo crate 的人都可以发布 foo::bar，而且只有他们可以，无需申请流程。

sentences.en: RFC 3243 explicitly avoids touching the existing hyphenated names (serde_json stays where it is; serde::json would be a new crate), so the 702 serde-* crates from many authors continue alongside anything the serde owners publish under serde::.

blocks.html_en: npm added @scope/name in 2014, and 37% of the 4.26 million live packages use one. Among the unscoped 63%, 68% share a prefix with at least four other packages. For the flat prefixes that also have a matching scope, the flat side is almost always larger, with the core team publishing under the scope and the community under the flat prefix: babel-* 6,099 vs @babel/* 239, vue-* 23,871 vs @vue/* 110, eslint-* 16,664 vs @eslint/* 17. The exceptions are generated families that migrated or started scoped, like @types/* (11,388) and @stdlib/* (5,555). eslint-plugin-* (6,957) and babel-plugin-* (3,987) stay flat because the tools string-match those prefixes when resolving config.

sentences.en: npm added @scope/name in 2014, and 37% of the 4.26 million live packages use one.
sentences.zh: npm 在 2014 年添加了 @scope/name，426 万个实时包中有 37% 使用了 @scope/name。

sentences.en: Among the unscoped 63%, 68% share a prefix with at least four other packages.
sentences.zh: 在未划定范围的 63% 中，68% 与至少四个其他软件包共享一个前缀。

sentences.en: For the flat prefixes that also have a matching scope, the flat side is almost always larger, with the core team publishing under the scope and the community under the flat prefix: babel-* 6,099 vs @babel/* 239, vue-* 23,871 vs @vue/* 110, eslint-* 16,664 vs @eslint/* 17.
sentences.zh: 对于也有匹配范围的扁平前缀，扁平面几乎总是更大，核心团队在该范围下发布，社区在扁平前缀下发布：babel-* 6,099 vs @babel/* 239，vue-* 23,871 vs @vue/* 110，eslint-* 16,664 vs @eslint/* 17。

sentences.en: The exceptions are generated families that migrated or started scoped, like @types/* (11,388) and @stdlib/* (5,555).
sentences.zh: 例外情况是迁移或开始限定范围的生成系列，例如 @types/* (11,388) 和 @stdlib/* (5,555)。

sentences.en: eslint-plugin-* (6,957) and babel-plugin-* (3,987) stay flat because the tools string-match those prefixes when resolving config.
sentences.zh: eslint-plugin-* (6,957) 和 babel-plugin-* (3,987) 保持不变，因为工具在解析配置时字符串匹配这些前缀。

blocks.html_en: Bulk publication appears in scopes as well as flat names: @hyper.fun alone has 30,273 packages, alongside a run of scopes with random-string names ending in npm (@wemnyelezxnpm, @diahkomalasarinpm, @ryniaubenpm) holding 1,500-1,900 packages each, all created in April 2024 during the tea.xyz protocol spam wave. dsr-* (10,742) and tea-* (4,486) from the same wave are unscoped.

blocks.html_en: The projects with the biggest plugin conventions maintain their own directories: jekyllrb.com/docs/plugins, homebridge.io, flow.nodered.org, n8n.io/integrations, fastlane’s plugin list. These are curated, so a package is listed because the project added it. Registry search returns fuzzy-ranked results across the whole index, so searching rubygems.org for jekyll gives popular jekyll-related gems mixed with anything else that mentions the word. NuGet’s checkmark and npm’s npmjs.com/org/babel scope page are the two places a registry currently exposes something membership-like.

sentences.en: The projects with the biggest plugin conventions maintain their own directories: jekyllrb.com/docs/plugins, homebridge.io, flow.nodered.org, n8n.io/integrations, fastlane’s plugin list.

Third-party registry browsers like npmx are well placed to try it for npm’s flat prefixes, and I have the cross-registry data on ecosyste.ms so I might build one there.

blocks.html_en: PEP 752’s four named prefixes cover about 2,000 PyPI packages from one organisation each, against about 25,000 under the open-convention prefixes named above. The companion policy PEP 755 is still in draft and sets out who can apply for a grant and on what terms, including a proposed queue priority for paid organisations. How it handles an application for django- or pytest-, from the project or from anyone else, is still to be worked out, and NuGet’s handling of Serilog.* is one existing precedent.

content_html_zh:
PEP 752 于 6 月底被接受。 它允许组织向 PyPI 申请保留包名称前缀，之后匹配该前缀的新上传将被拒绝，除非它们来自资助者。 现有的软件包已不再适用。 动机是模仿：有人将恶意软件发布为 google-cloud-something 或 apache-something，并在其前面加上供应商的名称。 PEP 命名了四个示例前缀：google-cloud-、opentelemetry-、apache-airflow-providers- 和 types-。

我从 PyPI 中提取了每个包名称（其中 912,665 个，通过 ecosyste.ms）并计算了前缀。 这四个包分别占 264、271、154 和 1,295 个包，并且每个包几乎完全由一个组织发布。 Google 发布 google-cloud-*，OpenTelemetry 项目发布 opentelemetry-*，Apache Airflow 团队发布 apache-airflow-providers-*，typeshed 维护者发布 types-*。 前缀保留正式化了这些出版商已经在做的事情； PEP 的作者之一 Jarek Potiuk 是 Airflow PMC 的成员。

PyPI 上有 13,934 个前缀由五个或更多包共享。 最大的是 django-*，有 17,450 个，由数千名不同作者发表，自 2010 年以来每年发布约 1000 个。 Flask-* 有 2,815 个，pytest-* 有 2,137 个，mkdocs-* 有 764 个，sphinxcontrib-* 有 370 个。 Collective.* 是 Plone 社区使用 Python 命名空间包机制的共享前缀，拥有 1,436 个包，并且至少从 2008 年开始使用。 任何人都可以将 Django 应用程序发布为 django-something，将 pytest 插件发布为 pytest-something，将 Sphinx 扩展发布为 sphinxcontrib-something，并且宿主项目的文档会告诉他们这样做。

PEP 752 的早期草案为此提供了一种模式，让赠款持有人将前缀标记为开放，以便任何人仍然可以在该前缀下发布，同时赠款持有人自己的软件包会获得视觉指示器。 它被删除是因为“动机不足，而且存储库在技术上可以通过标准授权语义满足此类用例”。 多个组织可以共享一项授权，该授权可以处理跨几个组织的前缀分割。 根据公认的设计，如果 Django 软件基金会保留 django-，现有的 17,450 个软件包将继续发布新版本，而原本将于明年发布的大约 1000 个新的 django-* 软件包将被阻止。 今年早些时候，我比较了各个注册中心的命名空间设计； NuGet、crates.io 和 npm 都已经在前缀上附加了一些内容。

七个登记处

在 -、_ 和 的运行中拆分包名称。 并按第一个标记分组：

  包 ≥5 个包的前缀 具有共享前缀的包 ≥100 ≥500
pypi.org 912,665 13,934 41% 427 66
crates.io 311,301 9,161 54% 166 19
rubygems.org 209,853 4,214 46% 132 18
hex.pm 22,373 382 32% 12 1
hackage.haskell.org 19,345 521 41% 9 0
npmjs.org（未限定范围） 2,678,188 37,460 68% 2,604 468
nuget.org 828,361 22,377 70% 690 72

npm 自 2014 年起就有了 @scope/name，因此它的行仅计算 63% 未划定范围的包。 每个注册中心的软件包中有三分之一到三分之二与至少四个其他软件包共享第一个令牌。 这里的计数仅是第一个令牌；像 apache-airflow-providers- 这样的多令牌前缀在表中计入 apache-* 下，并在文本中命名时单独计入。

开放插件约定（许多作者发布以主机项目命名的扩展）是每个注册表中最大前缀中最大的类别：django-* (17,450 pypi)、react-* (86,142 npm)、jekyll-* (1,658 rubygems)、bevy-* (1,870 crates)、phoenix_* (245 hex)、servant-* (152)黑客）。 其中一些是功能性的，这意味着名称必须与主机工具加载插件的模式相匹配：cargo 将cargo-foo 作为cargo foo 子命令运行，ESLint 的配置解析器匹配eslint-plugin-foo，pytest 通过入口点加载pytest-foo。

生成的 SDK 系列是第二大的，其中一个组织根据 API 规范发布了数百个包。 aws-sdk-*（473 个箱子、481 个 gem、92 个 pypi）、google-cloud-*（258 个箱子、510 个 gem、264 个 pypi、5 个 hackage）、azure-mgmt-*（273 个箱子、124 个 gem、334 个 pypi）。 Odoo 社区协会在 PyPI 上发布了大约 20,000 个软件包，涵盖七个版本前缀树（odoo8-addon-* 到 odoo14-addon-* 加上 odoo-addon-*），这是我在任何注册表中发现的最大的协调前缀使用。 在 npm 上，生成的系列采用范围：@types/* 11,388、@stdlib/* 5,555、@fontsource/* 2,122。

语言和框架标签占据了另一个大块：python-* (6,278)、rust-* (1,692)、ex_* (1,123 hex，Elixir 约定)、node-* (23,389 npm)、is-* (3,754 npm，谓词函数)、use-* (4,317 npm，React hooks)。 Hackage 有一组镜像模块路径（数据-*、网络-*、语言-*）。

入门指南会产生自己的前缀：rubygems 上的 Hola-* 有 764 个来自遵循 make your own gem 指南的人的包，PyPI 上的 example-package-* 和 example-pkg-* 有 1,031 个来自 Packaging.python.org 教程的包，而 crates.io 上的 Guesing-game-* 有 125 个来自 Rust 书第 2 章的包。 PyPI 上的 topsis-* 有 1,999 个软件包，名称中嵌入了学生编号（TOPSIS 是一种决策分析方法，被设置为编程作业），这些软件包似乎是自 2020 年以来每年运行的大学课程。

批量发布占据了最近一些最大的前缀：crates.io 上的 use-* 有 820 个包，其中 809 个是由一个帐户在 2026 年发布的。 PyPI 上的 iflow-mcp-* 拥有来自一个发布者的 2,505 个重新发布的 MCP 服务器包，另外还有来自同一帐户的 npm 上的 @iflow-mcp 下的 5,184 个包。 NuGet 上的 free.robux-* 有 3,620 个。 PyPI 上的 mcp-* 来自许多发布商，从 2023 年的 2 个软件包增加到 2026 年中期的 3,022 个软件包，达到这一数量的速度比 django-* 达到 3,000 个软件包的速度还要快。

对于下载量排名前 1,000 的软件包，crates.io、rubygems 和 pypi 上的 69% 到 80% 要么属于前缀集群，要么是大型集群命名的软件包（serde、pytest、rack）； hex 为 45%，hackage 为 60%。 在 ecosyste.ms 上标记为关键的软件包中，rubygems 的 974 个中的 226 个单独是 aws-sdk-*，74% 的 NuGet 位于 Microsoft.* 或 System.* 下。

NuGet

PEP 752 引用 NuGet 的 ID 前缀预留作为现有技术。 它于 2017 年 10 月上线，工作方式相同（通过电子邮件申请，除非来自预订持有者，否则匹配的上传将被拒绝，现有包将不受限制），并在库和 Visual Studio 中为经过验证的包添加复选标记。 NuGet 还支持子前缀委托（其中 Microsoft.* 持有者可以将 Microsoft.AspNet.* 委托给其他帐户）和公共保留（其中持有者在自己的包上打勾，而其他发布者仍然可以在前缀下上传，这就是 PEP 752 的早期草案所谓的开放命名空间）。

通过搜索 API 在前 30 个非垃圾邮件前缀中对 NuGet 的每个包验证标志进行采样，结果显示 Microsoft.*、DevExpress.*、Syncfusion.* 和 JetBrains.* 已完全验证且是单一发布商，而 Google.*、Amazon.* 和 Azure.* 则主要通过一些旧社区包进行验证。

Serilog.* 在 776 个包中得到了 6% 的验证，Serilog 是一个带有插件模型的日志库，其中接收器和丰富器由编写它们的人发布为 Serilog.Sinks.Foo 和 Serilog.Enrichers.Foo。 对其中的 60 个进行抽样并检查首次发布日期是否经过验证，核心团队的软件包（Serilog、Serilog.Sinks.File）经过验证，而社区软件包则未经验证，包括截至 2025 年每年首次发布的社区软件包（Serilog.Enrichers.Metrics.Memory，2025 年 9 月，所有者 Jandini）。 这与 Serilog.* 作为公共保留是一致的：八年来社区接收器仍然被接受。 Cake.*（607 个包，Cake 构建插件）在 7% 的验证中显示出相同的模式。 每个包都会出现复选标记；预订列表本身对于 NuGet 来说是私有的。

crates.io

Cargo 的 RFC 3243 已被接受并正在实施，采用了不同的方法：拥有 foo crate 的人都可以发布 foo::bar，而且只有他们可以，无需申请流程。 在具有 100 个或更多包的 166 个 crates.io 前缀中，156 个具有与第一个令牌匹配的裸板条箱，其中 37% 最后发布是在五年多前，20% 仅有一个版本。 async crate 有两个版本，最后一个版本是 2014 年，而 844 个 crate 被命名为 async-*。 google、aws 和 tree crates 均由与 Google、AWS 或 Tree-sitter 没有任何联系的个人拥有。 bevy、serde、tokio、tauri、actix、axum 和 windows 属于各自的项目。 RFC 3243 明确避免触及现有的连字符名称（serde_json 保留在原处；serde::json 将是一个新的板条箱），因此来自许多作者的 702 个 serde-* 板条箱将继续与 serde 所有者在 serde:: 下发布的任何内容一起使用。

npm

npm 在 2014 年添加了 @scope/name，426 万个实时包中有 37% 使用了 @scope/name。 在未划定范围的 63% 中，68% 与至少四个其他软件包共享一个前缀。 对于也有匹配范围的扁平前缀，扁平面几乎总是更大，核心团队在该范围下发布，社区在扁平前缀下发布：babel-* 6,099 vs @babel/* 239，vue-* 23,871 vs @vue/* 110，eslint-* 16,664 vs @eslint/* 17。 例外情况是迁移或开始限定范围的生成系列，例如 @types/* (11,388) 和 @stdlib/* (5,555)。 eslint-plugin-* (6,957) 和 babel-plugin-* (3,987) 保持不变，因为工具在解析配置时字符串匹配这些前缀。

批量发布出现在作用域和平面名称中：仅 @hyper.fun 就有 30,273 个包，以及一系列以 npm 结尾的随机字符串名称的作用域（@wemnyelezxnpm、@diahkomalasarinpm、@ryniaubenpm），每个作用域包含 1,500-1,900 个包，所有这些都是在 2024 年 4 月 tea.xyz 协议垃圾邮件浪潮期间创建的。 来自同一波的 dsr-* (10,742) 和 tea-* (4,486) 未纳入范围。

浏览

具有最大插件约定的项目维护自己的目录：jekyllrb.com/docs/plugins、homebridge.io、flow.nodered.org、n8n.io/integrations、fastlane 的插件列表。 这些都是精心策划的，因此会列出一个包，因为该项目添加了它。 注册表搜索会返回整个索引中的模糊排名结果，因此在 ruby​​gems.org 中搜索 jekyll 会得到与 jekyll 相关的流行 gem 以及任何其他提及该词的内容。 NuGet 的复选标记和 npm 的 npmjs.com/org/babel 范围页面是注册表当前公开类似会员资格的两个地方。

一个前缀页面列出了按依赖计数排名的每个 jekyll-* gem，并固定了 jekyll 本身并标记了其直接所有者的包，将获得这些项目目录提供的大部分内容，并且构建它的数据已经在注册表索引中。 像 npmx 这样的第三方注册表浏览器非常适合尝试 npm 的平面前缀，而且我在 ecosyste.ms 上有跨注册表数据，所以我可能会在那里构建一个。

PEP 755

PEP 752 的四个命名前缀分别涵盖来自一个组织的约 2,000 个 PyPI 包，而上述开放约定前缀下覆盖约 25,000 个。 配套政策 PEP 755 仍处于草案阶段，规定了谁可以申请补助金以及申请条件，包括为付费组织提议的排队优先权。 它如何处理来自项目或其他任何人的 django 或 pytest 应用程序仍有待解决，NuGet 对 Serilog.* 的处理是一个现有的先例。

数据和脚本：github.com/andrew/package-name-prefixes。
